# Full-Stack Web Development Skill

This skill helps you work through full-stack web development tasks in a structured way.
It is designed for workspace-level use to support application architecture, feature design, debugging, and deployment.

## Use When

- You want a step-by-step plan for building or improving a web app
- You need a clear workflow for frontend and backend development
- You want guidance on integrating APIs, databases, authentication, or deployment
- You want a checklist for code quality, testing, and production readiness

## What This Skill Produces

- A project workflow from idea to deploy
- A prioritized development plan with frontend, backend, data, and testing tasks
- Code review suggestions for HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and backend integration
- Deployment and hosting recommendations

## Workflow

1. Clarify requirements and scope
   - Identify the app type, user flows, tech stack, and success criteria
   - Determine whether the goal is static site, SPA, API-backed app, or full-stack service

2. Design the architecture
   - Define pages, components, services, and data models
   - Choose front-end structure, styling approach, routing, and state management
   - Choose backend type: serverless functions, REST API, GraphQL, or full web server

3. Implement frontend
   - Build HTML structure and responsive layout
   - Write accessible, maintainable CSS
   - Add behavior with JavaScript or chosen framework
   - Connect frontend to backend endpoints or data sources

4. Implement backend / integration
   - Design API routes, request/response contracts, and validation
   - Connect to a database or external API if needed
   - Add authentication and authorization when required

5. Test and validate
   - Verify UI behavior, form validation, and error handling
   - Test API endpoints and data persistence
   - Review performance, accessibility, and security

6. Deploy and monitor
   - Choose an appropriate hosting platform or deployment flow
   - Configure build and runtime settings
   - Add monitoring, error reporting, and documentation

## Completion Criteria

- Requirements and feature list are documented clearly
- User interface and interactions are implemented and responsive
- Backend integration works and data flows correctly
- Core functionality is tested and verified
- Deployment plan is defined and ready to execute

## Example Prompts

- "Use this skill to plan a full-stack portfolio site with a contact form and data storage."
- "Help me structure the frontend and backend for a small web app using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript."
- "Review my current project and suggest improvements for deployment readiness."
- "Create a checklist for converting this site into a full-stack application with an API."
